<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>The Chronicles of Minecraft | Elite Exhibition</title>
    <style>
        /* Elite Architecture Layout Reset */
        * {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
        }
        
        body {
            font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif;
            background-color: #020304;
            color: #f8fafc;
            line-height: 1.8;
            overflow-x: hidden;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
        }

        html {
            scroll-behavior: smooth;
        }

        /* Fixed Luxury Exhibition Navigation */
        header {
            position: fixed;
            top: 0;
            width: 100%;
            background: rgba(2, 3, 4, 0.9);
            backdrop-filter: blur(30px);
            -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(30px);
            z-index: 1000;
            border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(205, 161, 94, 0.08);
            animation: elegantFadeUp 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) both;
        }

        .nav-container {
            max-width: 1300px;
            margin: 0 auto;
            display: flex;
            justify-content: space-between;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 26px 40px;
        }

        .logo {
            font-size: 13px;
            font-weight: 900;
            color: #cda15e;
            letter-spacing: 7px;
            text-transform: uppercase;
        }

        nav a {
            color: #5c6775;
            text-decoration: none;
            margin-left: 40px;
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.75rem;
            letter-spacing: 3px;
            text-transform: uppercase;
            transition: color 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
        }

        nav a:hover {
            color: #cda15e;
        }

        /* Massive Asymmetric Canvas Welcome Block */
        .hero {
            height: 75vh;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            text-align: center;
            padding: 40px 30px;
            background: radial-gradient(circle at center, #0e1218 0%, #020304 100%);
            border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(205, 161, 94, 0.03);
        }

        .hero h1 {
            font-size: 5rem;
            font-weight: 900;
            margin-bottom: 25px;
            letter-spacing: -3px;
            color: #ffffff;
            line-height: 1.1;
            animation: elegantFadeUp 1s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) both;
        }

        .hero p {
            font-size: 1.3rem;
            max-width: 650px;
            color: #5c6775;
            margin-bottom: 50px;
            font-weight: 300;
            animation: elegantFadeUp 1.2s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) both;
        }

        .cta-btn {
            background-color: transparent;
            color: #cda15e;
            padding: 16px 45px;
            border: 1px solid #cda15e;
            border-radius: 0px;
            font-size: 0.75rem;
            font-weight: 700;
            text-transform: uppercase;
            letter-spacing: 4px;
            text-decoration: none;
            transition: all 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
            animation: elegantFadeUp 1.4s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) both;
        }

        .cta-btn:hover {
            background-color: #cda15e;
            color: #020304;
            box-shadow: 0 20px 40px rgba(205, 161, 94, 0.15);
        }

        /* Exhibition Timeline Container Block */
        .timeline-section {
            padding: 160px 0;
            max-width: 1000px;
            margin: 0 auto;
        }

        .section-title {
            text-align: center;
            font-size: 1.8rem;
            font-weight: 800;
            letter-spacing: 10px;
            text-transform: uppercase;
            margin-bottom: 160px;
            color: #cda15e;
        }

        .timeline-container {
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 160px;
        }

        /* Clean Flat Column Matrix without Obtrusive Middle Lines */
        .timeline-item {
            display: grid;
            grid-template-columns: 1.15fr 0.85fr;
            gap: 80px;
            align-items: center;
            animation: elegantFadeUp 1.6s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) both;
        }

        .timeline-item:nth-child(even) {
            grid-template-columns: 0.85fr 1.15fr;
        }

        .timeline-item:nth-child(even) .timeline-img-wrapper {
            order: 2;
        }

        /* Studio Quality Widescreen Framing Window */
        .timeline-img-wrapper {
            position: relative;
            border-radius: 0px;
            overflow: hidden;
            box-shadow: 0 35px 70px rgba(0,0,0,0.8);
            aspect-ratio: 16 / 10;
            background-color: #0a0e14;
            border: 1px solid rgba(205, 161, 94, 0.05);
            transition: all 0.6s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
        }

        .timeline-img-wrapper img {
            width: 100%;
            height: 100%;
            object-fit: cover;
            display: block;
            filter: grayscale(40%) brightness(0.75);
            transition: all 0.6s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
        }

        /* Premium Minimalist Content Presentation Grid */
        .meta-box {
            position: relative;
            padding: 10px 0;
        }

        .year-badge {
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 700;
            color: #5c6775;
            letter-spacing: 5px;
            text-transform: uppercase;
            font-family: SFMono-Regular, Consolas, monospace;
            display: inline-block;
            margin-bottom: 16px;
            position: relative;
        }

        /* Micro Interactive Gold Axis Line Under the Year */
        .year-badge::after {
            content: '';
            position: absolute;
            bottom: -6px;
            left: 0;
            width: 40px;
            height: 1px;
            background-color: rgba(205, 161, 94, 0.3);
            transition: all 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1);
        }

        .era-title {
            font-size: 2.4rem;
            font-weight: 700;
            color: #ffffff;
            margin-bottom: 22px;
            letter-spacing: -1px;
            line-height: 1.2;
        }

        .era-description {
            color: #5c6775;
            font-size: 1.05rem;
            text-align: justify;
            transition: color 0.4s ease;
        }

        /* ELITE INTERACTIVE HOVER REACTION INTERFACES */
        .timeline-item:hover .timeline-img-wrapper {
            transform: scale(1.02) translateY(-4px);
            box-shadow: 0 45px 90px rgba(0,0,0,0.95);
            border: 1px solid rgba(205, 161, 94, 0.15);
        }

        .timeline-item:hover .timeline-img-wrapper img {
            filter: grayscale(0%) brightness(0.9);
        }

        .timeline-item:hover .year-badge {
            color: #cda15e;
        }

        .timeline-item:hover .year-badge::after {
            width: 100%;
            background-color: #cda15e;
            box-shadow: 0 0 12px rgba(205, 161, 94, 0.6);
        }

        .timeline-item:hover .era-description {
            color: #94a3b8;
        }

        footer {
            background-color: #010102;
            padding: 80px 20px;
            text-align: center;
            border-top: 1px solid rgba(205, 161, 94, 0.04);
            color: #3b434e;
            font-size: 0.75rem;
            letter-spacing: 4px;
            text-transform: uppercase;
        }

        /* Premium Text Cascade Animation Keyframes */
        @keyframes elegantFadeUp {
            from {
                opacity: 0;
                transform: translateY(30px);
            }
            to {
                opacity: 1;
                transform: translateY(0);
            }
        }

        /* High-Definition Device Screen Adapter Queries */
        @media (max-width: 992px) {
            .timeline-item, .timeline-item:nth-child(even) {
                grid-template-columns: 1fr !important;
                gap: 40px;
            }
            .timeline-item:nth-child(even) .timeline-img-wrapper {
                order: 0;
            }
            .timeline-container { gap: 120px; padding: 0 30px; }
            .hero h1 { font-size: 3.2rem; }
            .era-title { font-size: 1.8rem; }
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>

    <header>
        <div class="nav-container">
            <div class="logo">MINECRAFT // CHRONOS</div>
            <nav>
                <a href="#hero">Overview</a>
                <a href="#timeline">Exhibition</a>
            </nav>
        </div>
    </header>

    <section class="hero" id="hero">
        <h1>The Story of a Sandbox</h1>
        <p>A beautifully curated, executive history archive documenting the major core engine overhauls and gameplay modifications across Minecraft history.</p>
        <a href="#timeline" class="cta-btn">Explore Archive</a>
    </section>
<br>
<br>
<br>
    <section class="timeline-section" id="timeline">
        <h2 class="section-title">The Chronological Index</h2>
        <div class="timeline-container">
                    <!-- Timeline Unit 1: 2009 Genesis -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Minecraft 2009 Era">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2009</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">The Coding Genesis & Cave Game</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">In May 2009, Swedish developer Markus "Notch" Persson built a primitive 3D block-building grid experiment called "Cave Game." Within days, this framework was launched publicly on online forums as Minecraft Classic. This historical phase featured raw block placement mechanics without survival parameters, allowing early internet users to spontaneously construct geometric worlds across endless flat planes.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 2: 2010 Indev & Alpha -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcSJ9TqavP8S3LptZIP4OgNqbItYlLTt36zgHQ&s" alt="Minecraft 2010 Era">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2010</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">Survival Infdev & Alpha Realism</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">The year 2010 introduced structural code overhauls named Indev and Infdev, stretching the game's terrain limits to near-infinite coordinates. The following Alpha expansion added core constraints like an absolute day-night lighting rotation, player health variables, the 3x3 desktop crafting grid, and iconic hostile enemies like the explosive Creeper. This shifted the project from a sandbox into a survival test loop.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 3: 2011 Full Launch -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Minecraft 2011 Launch">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2011</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">Official Launch 1.0 & The End</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">Minecraft left its extended public testing phases on November 18, 2011, making its retail debut at the inaugural Minecon assembly. This definitive update added the End Dimension along with the Ender Dragon fight, providing sandbox loops with a concrete completion state. It also implemented procedural village generation, complex potion brewing arrays, and experience-based enchanting trees.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 4: 2012 Scares & Mechanics -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="https://images.igdb.com/igdb/image/upload/t_cover_big_2x/co5jbm.jpg">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2012</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.4 - The Pretty Scary Update</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">Arriving in late 2012, this expansion focused extensively on horror elements, boss loops, and utility systems. It introduced the multi-stage Wither secondary boss fight alongside witches, bats, and zombie villagers. On top of that, it dropped essential mechanics like beacons for area-of-effect buffs, decorative item frames, and an overhaul to default trading pathways that gave villagers authentic economic value.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 5: 2013 Automation -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2013</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.5 & 1.6 - Redstone Automation</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">The 1.5 update, known as the Redstone Update, introduced critical automation components to the game, including hoppers, droppers, comparators, and daylight sensors. This allowed technical sandbox users to engineer fully automated sorting facilities and automated item farms. Shortly after, the 1.6 Horse Update added rideable horses and donkeys, which drastically improved map travel speeds across survival worlds.</p>
                </div>
            </div>
                        <!-- Timeline Unit 6: 2014 Acquisition -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2014</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.8 - The Bountiful Update & Transition</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">The year 2014 brought the launch of "The Bountiful Update" (version 1.8), expanding build choices by adding diorite, granite, and andesite stone types alongside procedural ocean monuments guarded by dangerous laser-firing Guardians. Simultaneously, the game's corporate structure was changed forever when tech giant Microsoft bought Mojang Studios and the entire Minecraft franchise for $2.5 billion, paving the way for global cross-play infrastructure optimization.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 7: 2015 Developer Shift -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2015</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">The Windows 10 & Engine Re-Code</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">The year 2015 was defined by major structural behind-the-scenes re-coding. Microsoft launched the "Minecraft: Windows 10 Edition" Beta, introducing the C++ Bedrock Engine alongside the original Java framework. This allowed players on PC, mobile devices, and consoles to eventually interact on unified multiplayer servers. Simultaneously, technical snapshots laid the groundwork for a total rework of the survival combat loop.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 8: 2016 Combat & Exploration -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2016</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.9 & 1.11 - Elytra & Woodland Mansions</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">After an intensive code-rebuilding period, 2016 delivered two major gameplay additions. The Combat Update (1.9) completely rebalanced survival fight mechanics, introduced defensive shields, and expanded the outer islands of the End with Elytra glider wings. Later that year, the Exploration Update (1.11) dropped rare Woodland Mansions guarded by dangerous spell-casting Illagers, alongside inventory-saving Shulker Boxes.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 9: 2017 Color World -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2017</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.12 - The World of Color Update</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">Arriving in mid-2017, the World of Color update heavily modernized creative tools for sandbox builders. It introduced a vibrant palette overhaul via hardened concrete, concrete powder, glazed terracotta patterns, and newly colored sleeping beds. This drop also stripped out the old rigid achievement list, replacing it with the dynamic branching Advancement pathway alongside a helpful in-game crafting recipe book.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 10: 2018 Aquatic Overhaul -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2018</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.13 - The Massive Update Aquatic</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">In July 2018, the Update Aquatic (version 1.13) completely transformed the game's oceans from empty water blocks into complex, living ecosystems. This patch dropped colorful coral reefs, hidden shipwrecks, dolphins, and sea turtles into marine biomes. It also completely upgraded water physics, introduced a fluid 3D swimming animation, and added the heavy trident weapon for underwater combat encounters.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 11: 2019 Village Overhauls -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2019</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.14 - Village & Pillage Civilization</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">The Village & Pillage update launched in April 2019, completely reworking village generation layouts to match separate biomes. It introduced specific workstations like blast furnaces and smokers that gave villagers professions, alongside a complex economy trading network. It also added Pillager Outposts, the Bad Omen effect, dynamic raid mechanics, crossbows, foxes, and pandas.</p>
                </div>
            </div>
            <!-- Timeline Unit 12: 2020 Underworld -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2020</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.16 - The Nether Biome Ecosystems</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">In 2020, the transformative Nether Update turned the game's underworld dimension from a barren wasteland into a vibrant ecosystem. This drop added distinctive biomes like Crimson Forests, Soulsand Valleys, and Basalt Deltas, new piglin tribes, and netherite—a rare, fireproof alloy mined from ancient debris that surpassed diamond as the most durable tier of armor and tools in survival worlds.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 13: 2021 Cavern Elevation -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2021</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.17 & 1.18 - Caves & Cliffs Overhauls</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">Split across two massive updates, Caves & Cliffs completely broke and rebuilt the core world-generation engine. It pushed building limits higher into the clouds and deep below bedrock to generate massive cavern biomes like Lush and Dripstone Caves. It also populated the world with raw copper veins, amethyst geodes, goats, and adorable axolotls.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 14: 2022 The Deep Dark -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2022</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.19 - The Deep Dark & The Warden</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">The Wild Update introduced a terrifying subterranean biome known as the Deep Dark. Spawning deep beneath the mountain roots, this eerie expanse featured forgotten Ancient Cities guarded by the blind, sound-tracking Warden boss creature. It also brought mangrove swamp forests, mud bricks, boat chest variants, and frogs to the overworld surface ecosystem.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 15: 2023 Self Expression -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2023</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.20 - Trails & Tales & Archeology</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">Focusing heavily on self-expression, storytelling, and custom aesthetics, version 1.20 added brush-driven archeology features to desert temples and ocean ruins. Players gained the ability to sift for ancient pottery shards, decorate armor plates via customizable armor smithing templates, navigate using rideable camels, and explore the tranquil pink Cherry Grove biomes.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 16: 2024 Automation Trials -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2024</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.21 - Tricky Trials & The Crafter</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">Tricky Trials expanded mid-game combat challenges by introducing procedural, copper-dense Trial Chambers filled with automated spawners and breeze mobs. Most importantly, it added the revolutionary redstone-driven Crafter block. The Crafter block allows technical engineers to fully automate item crafting using puzzle wiring patterns, checking off a community request over a decade old.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 17: 2025 Pale Garden -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2025</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">1.22 - The Eerie Pale Garden Drop</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">Moving away from slow annual updates toward a continuous drop format, Mojang launched the creepy Pale Garden biome. This monochromatic forest completely goes silent at night, spawning a wooden hostile creature known as the Creaking. The Creaking cannot take regular damage unless players locate and destroy its hidden sculk-like heart node tucked inside neighboring pale oak trees.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

            <!-- Timeline Unit 18: 2026 Modern Horizon -->
            <div class="timeline-item">
                <div class="timeline-img-wrapper">
                    <img src="data:image/jpeg;base64,">
                </div>
                <div class="meta-box">
                    <div class="year-badge">Chronos // 2026</div>
                    <h3 class="era-title">Modern Modular Game Drops/tiny takeover</h3>
                    <p class="era-description">
                    The Tiny Takeover update—officially released in early 2026 as version 26.1—serves as Minecraft's first "Game Drop" of the year. This patch delivers a massive visual overhaul to over 30 baby mobs, replacing their old, downscaled adult models with entirely unique textures and adorable animations. To complement these changes, players can craft a golden dandelion to permanently halt a baby animal's aging process so it stays small forever. The update also streamlines inventory management by making name tags fully craftable using just paper and a metal nugget. Finally, builders gain a few helpful utility tweaks, including functional trumpet note blocks made from copper and expanded stonecutter recipes.
<br>
                     Entering its seventeenth year, Minecraft continues as the best-selling video game of all time. Modern performance enhancements, cloud engine lighting re-codes, and smaller, quarterly update packages keep the sandbox highly optimized across mobile platforms, virtual reality ecosystems, and high-end gaming computers, ensuring the universe evolves with the creative desires of its players.</p>
                </div>
            </div>

        </div>
    </section>

    <!-- Professional Architecture Exhibition Footer -->
    <footer>
        <p>&copy; 2026 Minecraft Chronos Exhibition. Designed by thegamemaster09.</p>
    </footer>

</body>
</html>
